title: A paired prototype and focused survey can validate value before integration.
narrative_role: Survey, Google Form plan, experiment design, and future charts
objective: Define how the problem and solution will be validated, reserve chart areas, and prevent fabricated survey claims.

layout.template: Validation ladder plus three chart placeholders
layout.left: Research sequence and paired test design
layout.right: Google Form link placeholder and chart frames
layout.footer: All survey charts remain blank until responses are collected

validation_sequence.sample: 5–6 active discovery seekers including 1–2 positive counterexamples
validation_sequence.decision: Does current-session intent/control appear in recent behavior?
Can at least 4/5 set intent, understand novelty, correct fields, and steer unaided?
Does guided discovery improve accepted novel-artist rate without lowering relevance?

paired_experiment.baseline: Same taste anchor, catalog, card count, and layout; no free-text intent, novelty control, or iterative steering
paired_experiment.guided: Approved intent, novelty, exclusions, explanations, and immediate steering
paired_experiment.guardrail: Overall acceptance and perceived relevance must not decline materially

survey_plan.note: The questionnaire is ready for later Google Form creation. This deck must not imply responses exist.

chart_placeholders.title: Would users take one short intent step?

visuals.alt: Validation ladder progressing from problem interviews to usability, paired prototype, and a product decision.

speaker_notes: Do not create a pie chart from a multi-select question; use a horizontal bar for failure modes.
Survey results are directional context, while the paired prototype tests behavior.
Insert the live Google Form hyperlink only after the form is created and verified.
